summary:
Improvements in survival among women with gynaecological cancers in the Anglia region are strongly associated with multidisciplinary team involvement, which enhances diagnostic accuracy, personalized treatments, and coordinated patient pathways. The potential role of centralized care is supported by its ability to provide greater access to specialized expertise and standardized workflow practices within a unified system.

The summary above meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focused exclusively on clinically significant factors related directly to improved survival through teamwork or systemic organization.
